root_text.iti82:2.1: “Tayome, bhikkhave, devesu devasaddā niccharanti samayā samayaṁ upādāya.
root_text.iti82:2.3: Yasmiṁ, bhikkhave, samaye ariyasāvako kesamassuṁ ohāretvā kāsāyāni vatthāni acchādetvā agārasmā anagāriyaṁ pabbajjāya ceteti, tasmiṁ samaye devesu devasaddo niccharati:
root_text.iti82:2.5: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, paṭhamo devesu devasaddo niccharati samayā samayaṁ upādāya.
root_text.iti82:3.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, yasmiṁ samaye ariyasāvako sattannaṁ bodhipakkhiyānaṁ dhammānaṁ bhāvanānuyogamanuyutto viharati, tasmiṁ samaye devesu devasaddo niccharati:
root_text.iti82:3.3: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, dutiyo devesu devasaddo niccharati samayā samayaṁ upādāya.
root_text.iti82:4.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, yasmiṁ samaye ariyasāvako āsavānaṁ khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharati, tasmiṁ samaye devesu devasaddo niccharati:
root_text.iti82:4.2: ‘eso ariyasāvako vijitasaṅgāmo tameva saṅgāmasīsaṁ abhivijiya ajjhāvasatī’ti.
root_text.iti82:4.3: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, tatiyo devesu devasaddo niccharati samayā samayaṁ upādāya.
root_text.iti82:4.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, tayo devesu devasaddā niccharanti samayā samayaṁ upādāyā”ti.
root_text.iti82:8.1: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.

translation_text.iti82:1.1: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.
translation_text.iti82:2.1: “Mendicants, these three cries are uttered among the gods on occasion.
translation_text.iti82:2.3: When a noble disciple shaves off their hair and beard, dresses in ocher robes, and goes forth from the lay life to homelessness, the gods cry out:
translation_text.iti82:2.4: ‘This noble disciple intends to join battle with Māra!’
translation_text.iti82:2.5: This is the first occasion a cry is uttered among the gods.
translation_text.iti82:3.1: Furthermore, when a noble disciple meditates pursuing the development of the seven qualities that lead to awakening, the gods cry out:
translation_text.iti82:3.2: ‘This noble disciple is joining battle with Māra!’
translation_text.iti82:3.3: This is the second occasion a cry is uttered among the gods.
translation_text.iti82:4.1: Furthermore, when a noble disciple realizes the undefiled freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom in this very life, and they live having realized it with their own insight due to the ending of defilements, the gods cry out:
translation_text.iti82:4.2: ‘This noble disciple has won victory in battle, establishing himself as foremost in battle!’
translation_text.iti82:4.3: This is the third occasion a cry is uttered among the gods.
translation_text.iti82:4.4: These are the three cries that are uttered among the gods on occasion.”
translation_text.iti82:6.2: You won a battle hard to win!
translation_text.iti82:6.3: Having defeated the army of death,
translation_text.iti82:7.1: And so the deities revere the one,
translation_text.iti82:7.2: who has achieved their heart’s desire.
translation_text.iti82:7.3: For they see nothing in them by means of which
translation_text.iti82:7.4: they might fall under the sway of Death.”
translation_text.iti82:8.1: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.

variant_text.iti82:2.3: pabbajjāya ceteti, tasmiṁ samaye → tasmiṁ bhikkhave samaye (pts-vp-pli1, mr)
